title: Security guards and related occupations
description: Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents. Assist lawyers by investigating facts, preparing legal documents, or researching legal precedent. Conduct research to support a legal proceeding, to formulate a defense, or to initiate legal action. Search real estate records, examine titles, or summarize pertinent legal or insurance documents or details for a variety of purposes. May compile lists of mortgages, contracts, and other instruments pertaining to titles by searching public and private records for law firms, real estate agencies, or title insurance companies. (Based on 28 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Dress with equipment such as fire-resistant clothing and breathing apparatus; clean and maintain fire stations and firefighting equipment; take action to contain any hazardous chemicals that could catch fire, leak, or spill; extinguish flames and embers to suppress fires, using shovels or engine- or hand-driven water or chemical pumps; administer first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation to injured persons; rescue survivors from various hazards and inform and educate the public on fire prevention.

Operate pumps connected to high-pressure hoses; salvage property by removing broken glass, pumping out water, and ventilating buildings to remove smoke; participate in physical training activities to maintain physical fitness; prepare written reports detailing specifics of fire incidents; maintain knowledge of current firefighting practices through drills, seminars, and conferences; inspect buildings for fire hazards and compliance with fire prevention ordinances.

Inspect fire sites after flames have been extinguished to ensure no further danger; participate in fire drills and demonstrations; collaborate with police during accidents, disasters, and arson investigations; patrol burned areas to locate and eliminate hot spots; maintain contact with fire dispatchers to report needs; assess fires and report conditions to superiors; respond to fire alarms and calls for assistance.

Drive and operate firefighting vehicles and equipment; position and climb ladders for access and rescues; pump water onto fires using appropriate methods; create openings in buildings using tools; monitor and adjust controls that regulate building systems, such as air conditioning.

Patrol work areas to detect suspicious items; lock doors and gates to secure buildings; monitor and authorize entrance and departure of individuals to prevent theft; inform other screeners about baggage that should not be opened; challenge suspicious people and request identification; notify supervisors when security breaches occur.

Prepare reports of daily activities and irregularities, including thefts and unauthorized persons; follow individuals who breach security; patrol industrial or commercial premises to prevent intrusion; maintain order and adhere to safety rules; issue supplies and equipment to workers; inspect work performed to ensure compliance with standards and specifications.

Communicate with police in cases of urgent security issues; inspect and adjust security systems to ensure operational use; escort individuals to specified locations, providing personal protection as needed; advise managers of facility readiness; evaluate employee performance and recommend actions accordingly; coordinate activities with other departments to ensure service efficiency.

Prepare records regarding budgets, payrolls, and other operational information; plan and prepare employee work schedules; maintain required records of work hours; inventory stock; recommend changes to improve service and efficiency; request additional services for facility maintenance; supervise cleaning duties as necessary.

Respond to medical emergencies by administering first aid; perform maintenance on items like cars and facilities as needed; investigate complaints and take corrective actions; establish operational standards and procedures for supervised departments.
Prepare and manage budgets, estimate costs; clean and inspect facilities; perform supplemental duties related to customer service and facility maintenance as necessary.

Clean and polish footwear, operate washing machines for cleaning; provide assistance to patrons and manage inventory; perform other supplementary duties like directing patrons or managing kiosks; operate controls that regulate environmental systems in facilities.

Perform grounds maintenance, assist athletic teams; support the setup and operation of projection equipment and evaluate performance; oversee ticket verification and verify patron credentials; engaging with patrons on questions and concerns.

Search for lost articles or parents of lost children, assist patrons in finding seats and settling disputes, and provide customer assistance and information including directions to points in or outside of the facility.

Monitor compliance with standard operating procedures for loss prevention, physical security, or risk management, conduct personal background investigations, and perform covert surveillance of areas susceptible to loss.

Inspect buildings, equipment, or access points to determine security risks, investigate known or suspected theft, and maintain documentation or reports on security-related incidents or investigations.
Collaborate with law enforcement agencies to report or investigate crimes, and provide assistance to other local law enforcement agencies as required.
Apprehend shoplifters in accordance with guidelines and arrest persons in court when arrest warrants have been issued.
Seize equipment used in law violations, issue warnings or citations, and prepare written reports on investigations.
Screen, control, and handle evidence and exhibits during court proceedings, provide security by patrolling the courthouse, and check courtroom for security and cleanliness.
Meet with clients and other professionals to discuss details of cases, prepare for trial by organizing exhibits, and prepare legal documents such as affidavits and legal correspondence.
Research laws, court decisions, and other information related to cases before the court, and prepare briefs, legal memoranda, or statements of issues involved in cases.
Attend court sessions to hear oral arguments, and testify in civil or criminal proceedings when necessary.
Confer with judges concerning legal questions and review complaints, petitions, or motions that have been filed to determine issues involved.
Train new or temporary staff and collaborate with different departments to assist in investigations, training, or company programs.
Keep abreast of changes in the law and inform judges when cases are affected by such changes, and address schools and civic groups to disseminate information concerning regulations.
Compile and present evidence for court actions and prepare periodic reports on court proceedings as required.
Maintain judges' law libraries by assembling or updating appropriate documents, and supervise law students or volunteers assigned to the court.
Participate in conferences between trial attorneys and judges regarding case management and procedural requirements.
Perform courtroom duties, including calling calendars and administering oaths, and enter information into computerized court management systems.
Maintain order in courtroom and guard jury from outside contact.
Guard lodging of sequestered jury and provide jury escort to restaurant and other areas outside of courtroom to prevent jury contact with the public.
Enforce courtroom rules of behavior, including warning persons not to smoke or disturb court procedures, and stopping people from entering the courtroom while the judge charges the jury.
Report the need for police or medical assistance to the sheriff's office, and contact emergency medical personnel in case of serious injury.
Announce the entrance of the judge and maintain access to, and security of, highly sensitive materials.
Determine response requirements and relative priorities of situations, dispatch units in accordance with established procedures, and record details of calls, dispatches, and messages.
Question callers to determine their locations and the nature of their problems, and provide emergency medical instructions to callers as necessary.
Relay information and messages to and from emergency sites, law enforcement agencies, and all other individuals or groups requiring notification.
Patrol or monitor recreational areas, such as trails, slopes, or swimming areas, to prevent or detect intrusion, protect property, or preserve order.
Receive incoming telephone or alarm system calls regarding emergency and non-emergency services, maintaining files of information relating to emergency calls.
Monitor various radio frequencies to stay apprised of developing situations and observe alarm registers and scan maps to determine whether a specific emergency is in the dispatch service area.
Test and adjust communication and alarm systems, monitor alarm systems to detect emergencies, and report malfunctions to maintenance units.

Examine individual titles, verify accuracy and completeness of land-related documents accepted for registration, and prepare reports describing any title encumbrances encountered during searching activities.

Prepare, edit, or review legal documents, including legislation, briefs, pleadings, appeals, wills, contracts, and real estate closing statements, and confer with realtors and relevant personnel to exchange title-related information.
Write reports documenting investigations, including observations made while on patrol, and maintain required records, including logs of parking enforcement activities or investigations.
Monitor the behavior of security employees to ensure adherence to quality standards and develop and document security procedures, policies, or standards.
Train security personnel on protective procedures, first aid, fire safety, and other duties, and schedule training or drills for emergencies, such as fires, bombs, and other threats.
Apprehend or evict trespassers, rule violators, or other security threats from the premises, and report all violations and suspicious behaviors to supervisors.

Monitor establishment activities to ensure adherence to all state gaming regulations and company policies, and inspect and monitor audio or video surveillance equipment to ensure it is working appropriately.
Act as oversight or security agents for management or customers and observe and report hazardous conditions.
Provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations during the execution of duties, and explain company policies and procedures to staff.

Supervise and participate in the inspection of properties to ensure compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, laws, regulations, and standards, and evaluate fire station procedures to ensure efficiency and enforcement of departmental regulations.
Analyze burn conditions and results, and prepare postburn reports, as well as plan, direct, and supervise prescribed burn projects.

Study and interpret fire safety codes to establish procedures for issuing permits to handle hazardous or flammable substances, and inspect and test fire protection and detection systems to ensure proper operation.
Direct investigations of suspected arson, hazards, and false alarms, and submit reports outlining findings.
Perform administrative duties such as compiling and maintaining records, preparing reports, and composing correspondence.
Recommend personnel actions related to disciplinary procedures, performance, leaves of absence, and grievances, and schedule employee work assignments and set work priorities.
Direct the training of firefighters and assign instructors to training classes, while evaluating the performance of assigned firefighting personnel.
Drive crew carriers to transport firefighters to incidents and maintain required maps and records.

Inspect uniforms, equipment, and recreation areas to ensure compliance with safety standards, taking corrective action as necessary, and perform maintenance and minor repairs on firefighting equipment, including writing proposals for modifications or repairs.
Participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes for nonresidential buildings, and monitor fire suppression expenditures to ensure necessity and reasonableness.

Point of sale POS computer terminals

emerging_tasks.task: Download digital keys to unlock digital movie files.
Ingest digital content, such as films, advertisements, or trailers, into servers or projectors.
Deliver educational presentations on wildlife topics, such as animal safety, at schools and community events.
Manage attorneys' calendars and schedule meetings.
Operate drones for surveillance of large areas and tracking of wildlife.
Perform traffic control duties such as setting up barricades and temporary signs, placing bags on parking meters to limit their use, or directing traffic or pedestrians.
Pick up deceased animals from roadways and dispose of the animal carcasses.
Request, review, and summarize relevant records for the cases.
Serve documents to parties named in legal proceedings.
Testify at court hearings when citations are contested.
Transport injured or ill wildlife to rescues.
Use advanced technology, such as drones, GPS trackers, and surveillance cameras, to facilitate investigations.
Use drone technology for surveillance and loss prevention.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
